Professional Loan Calculator

Calculate your commercial finance repayments with our professional calculator suite. Get instant estimates for equipment finance, commercial property, and working capital solutions with Australian tax considerations.

✓ Australian Financial Calculations ✓ GST Inclusive Options ✓ Professional Assessment Available

Equipment Finance Calculator

Calculate repayments for commercial equipment, vehicles, and machinery financing.

Minimum $10,000 - Maximum $5,000,000
Indicative rate - actual rates vary by application
Optional - reduces loan amount

Commercial Property Calculator

Calculate repayments for commercial property purchase or investment financing.

Minimum $100,000 - Maximum $20,000,000
Commercial property rates typically 6-10%

Working Capital Calculator

Calculate repayments for business line of credit and working capital facilities.

Minimum $25,000 - Maximum $2,000,000
Working capital rates typically 10-18%
Monthly fee on total facility limit

Invoice Financing Calculator

Calculate costs for invoice factoring and debtor financing to improve cash flow.

Minimum $50,000 - Maximum $5,000,000
Monthly factor rate typically 1.5-4%
Monthly service fee on turnover

Additional Information
Get Professional Assessment

Understanding Your Calculations

Interest Rate Factors

Commercial finance rates vary based on loan type, security, business strength, and market conditions. The rates shown are indicative only.

  • Equipment finance: 6-15% typically
  • Commercial property: 6-10% typically
  • Working capital: 10-18% typically

GST Considerations

For equipment purchases, GST may be claimable as an input tax credit if you're GST registered, effectively reducing the net equipment cost.

  • GST registered businesses can claim GST credits
  • Consider net equipment cost for financing
  • Consult your accountant for GST implications

Additional Costs

Commercial finance may include additional costs not reflected in basic calculations:

  • Establishment fees ($500-$2,500)
  • Valuation costs ($300-$1,500)
  • Legal documentation fees
  • Ongoing account keeping fees

Invoice Financing Benefits

Invoice financing can significantly improve cash flow by providing immediate access to working capital:

  • Immediate access to 70-90% of invoice value
  • Improves cash flow for growth opportunities
  • No fixed monthly repayments required
  • Scales with your business turnover

Comparing Finance Options

Different finance types suit different business needs and circumstances:

  • Bank loans: Lower rates, stricter criteria
  • Alternative lenders: Faster approval, higher rates
  • Equipment finance: Asset-secured, competitive rates
  • Invoice finance: Flexible, turnover-based

Professional Assessment Benefits

Our professional assessment provides accurate rates and terms based on your specific situation:

  • Personalised interest rate assessment
  • Optimal loan structure recommendations
  • Tax efficiency considerations
  • Multiple lender comparison

Important Information & Disclaimers

Calculation Accuracy: These calculations are estimates only and should not be relied upon for final decision making. Actual loan terms, interest rates, and repayments will vary based on your individual circumstances, credit assessment, and lender requirements.

Interest Rates: Interest rates shown are indicative only and based on current market conditions. Actual rates offered will depend on loan type, security, loan amount, term, and borrower profile. Rates are subject to change without notice.

Professional Advice Required: This calculator does not constitute financial advice. You should seek independent financial, legal, and tax advice before making any borrowing decisions. Consider your objectives, financial situation, and needs before proceeding.

Australian Financial Services: Edgeview Finance Business Solutions is a licensed finance broker operating under Australian Financial Services License conditions. We are required to act in your best interests when providing credit assistance.

No Guarantee: Use of this calculator does not constitute a loan pre-approval or guarantee of finance. All finance applications are subject to lender credit assessment and approval processes.
